identity.canonical_title: Bonus: Vector Pyramid Meets Momentum Space: p_μ p^μ

theory.canonical_statement: Since the Klein–Gordon equation originates from: \[ p_\mu p^\mu = (mc)^2, \] you could equally build your pyramid in **momentum space**:
theory.technical_interpretation: Bonus: Vector Pyramid Meets Momentum Space: p_μ p^μ is an algebraic definition, written p_μ p^μ = (mc)^2. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from m, c, defining p_μ p^μ. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where since the Klein–Gordon equation originates from
